findings.description: SKILL.md uses description-only framing patterns documented in Saha et al. (arXiv:2605.11418) §6 — claims of being 'best', 'always use', 'trusted by', 'source of truth' etc. — that bias an agent's selection in 77.6% of pairwise trials even when the underlying capability is unchanged. Strong manipulation surface for supply-chain attackers.

  26 | Required posture:
  27 | - preserve Stitch structure unless a runtime hook requires a small change
» 28 | - keep shared app behavior in root `app.js`; do not fork manager/admin logic into route-local scripts
  29 | - keep settings-route auth, role gating, save-vs-send, notifications, featured management, and menu switching intact
  30 | - only add controls Stitch omitted when the app truly requires them

findings.snippet: - keep shared app behavior in root `app.js`; do not fork manager/admin logic into route-local scripts
